$shuffle_seed_derivation: ASCII sum of '4.2' = 52+46+50 = 148; deterministic across runs

questions.objective_ref: Define fine-tuning in plain language and explain how it differs from training a model from scratch.
Explain what domain-specific data means and give two examples of a domain and its corresponding data.
Describe the general sequence of steps involved in fine-tuning a foundation model.
Identify at least two real-world scenarios where fine-tuning is the appropriate adaptation choice.
Explain the trade-off between fine-tuning and using a foundation model without any adaptation (zero-shot use).
Recognise when fine-tuning may not be the right tool — for example, when the task changes frequently or when labelled data is unavailable.
